---
title: "Notes S-01/Excel 1: Basics, formulas, charts, conditionals"
session_id: session-01
event_id: null
---

| Time                 | Topic/activity                                             | Materials                                                            |
| -------------------: | ---------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 0–15 min             | Introduction and organization                              | [Introductory slides](../slides/session_00_orga.qmd) (separate)      |
| 15–35 min            | Excel basics, formulas, and cell references                | Slides 1–6<br>                                                       |
| 35–55 min            | Future-value calculation                                   | Slides 7–10<br>Slides 7 and 10: Exercise **Future value**            |
| 55–70 min            | Data handling and chart selection                          | Slides 11–14<br>Slide 14: Exercise **Coffee-shop charts**            |
| 70–90 min            | Functions and descriptive statistics                       | Slides 15–19<br>Slide 19: Exercise **Sales statistics**              |
| **Break**            |                                                            |                                                                      |
| 0–15 min             | Explain the moving-average task and expected output        | Slide 20<br>Take-home exercise **DAX moving averages**               |
| 15–35 min            | Conditional logic and customer classification              | Slides 21–23<br>Slide 23: Exercise **VIP status**                    |
| 35–50 min            | Conditional calculations                                   | Slide 24: Exercise **VIP club credits**                              |
| 50–70 min            | Work through the installment-loan calculation on the board | Slide 25: Take-home exercise **Installment loan**                    |
| 70–82 min            | Annuity loans and the `PMT` function                       | Slide 26: Exercise **Annuity loan**                                  |
| 82–90 min            | Exam-result classification and wrap-up                     | Slides 27–28<br>Slide 27: Exercise **Exam result**                   |

## Slide 1 - Excel basics

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class demo]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-demo} [`../materials/introduction.xlsx`](../materials/introduction.xlsx){.teaching-button .teaching-button-demo} -- Sheet: Start
:::
<!-- see [solution](../materials/introduction_with_solutions.xlsx) -->

![](../images/Excel_introduction_xlsx_1.png){fig-align="center" width="50%"}

- Entering formulas in the cell or the bar
- Selecting cells by typing "=A1+A2" vs. by clicking on cells
- Some cells are used for entering data, others for formulas (we need to understand which type of cell is needed)
- Illustrate data types: Add "€" to the nubmers and show that the SUM/EXP no longer work. 
- Anticipate changes (based on discussions/feedback), extensions of Excel sheets (need for proper design).
  Use clear labels (e.g., "Price of coffee", "Price of tea" instead of Number 1, Number 2).
  Make sure that formulas continue to work correctly if we add items (like "Price of water").
  For this, we would need to adapt the SUM formula. Some may already know how to do this, and we will come back to it later today.

::: {.callout-tip title="Pro tips"}

Expect that Excel spreadsheets will contain errors.
Always check and debug: for the exercises, but also in your job, when you build an Excel spreadsheet or when you get one from a colleague.

- Show formula mode
- Shortcut: `ctrl`+`shift`+`` ` ``
- Show predecessor/successor visually
- Show calculations (stopped?)
:::

## Slide 7: Future Value (first exercise)

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class exercise]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-exercise} [`../materials/Excel_in_class_exercises.xlsx`](../materials/Excel_in_class_exercises.xlsx){.teaching-button .teaching-button-exercise} -- Sheet: Future_Value
:::

Task: Create a first spreadsheet and create the formula for future value.

Solution:

![](../images/Excel_future_value_1.png){fig-align="center" width="50%"}

::: {.callout-tip title="Pro tips"}
Use keyboard instead of clicking (prepare/mention shortcuts)
:::

## Slide 8: Referencing cells (example: cost revenue calculation)

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class demo]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-demo} [`../materials/introduction.xlsx`](../materials/introduction.xlsx){.teaching-button .teaching-button-demo} -- Sheet: Cost_Revenue
:::

Solution:

![](../images/excel_cost_profit_calculation.png){fig-align="center" width="80%"}

## Slide 10 - Future Value (second exercise)

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class exercise]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-exercise} [`../materials/Excel_in_class_exercises.xlsx`](../materials/Excel_in_class_exercises.xlsx){.teaching-button .teaching-button-exercise} -- Sheet: Future_Value2
:::

Solution:

![](../images/future_value_abs_rel_refs.png){fig-align="center" width="80%"}

## Slide 13 - Charts illustration

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class demo]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-demo} [`../materials/introduction.xlsx`](../materials/introduction.xlsx){.teaching-button .teaching-button-demo} -- Sheet: with_plot
:::

![](../images/excel_charts_example.png){fig-align="center" width="90%"}

## Slide 14 - Exercise Coffee-shop charts

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class exercise]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-exercise} [`../materials/Excel_in_class_exercises.xlsx`](../materials/Excel_in_class_exercises.xlsx){.teaching-button .teaching-button-exercise} -- Sheet: CoffeeShop
:::

![](../images/excel_coffeeshop_charts.png){fig-align="center" width="90%"}

## Slide 16 — Formula syntax and functions

- Show graphical Formulas selection (areas: ...)
- Demonstrate the function-argument separator.
- Examples (at the bottom): how many parameters are there in each function?
- Explicitly distinguish between a **formula** and a **function**.


::: {.callout-important title="Test Excel configuration"}
Test requirement: **For `SUM(3,5)`: everyone should have `8` as the result.**
This ensures that language is set to English and parameters are separated by `,`.

- English: Make sure Excel is in English (File -> Options -> Language; restart)
- Separator:

    1. Close Excel.
    2. Press **`Win` + `R`**.
    3. Enter **`intl.cpl`** and press Enter.
    4. Click **Additional settings… / Weitere Einstellungen…**
    5. Under **Numbers / Zahlen**, set:

    * **Decimal symbol / Dezimaltrennzeichen:** `.`
    * **Digit grouping symbol:** `,`
    * **List separator / Listentrennzeichen:** `,`
    6. Confirm with **OK** and restart Excel.

    In Excel, ensure **File → Options → Advanced → Use system separators** remains enabled.

:::

## Slide 18 - Formula illustration

![](../images/excel_formula_illustration.png){fig-align="center" width="90%"}

## Slide 19 - Sales statistics

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class exercise]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-exercise} [`../materials/Excel_in_class_exercises.xlsx`](../materials/Excel_in_class_exercises.xlsx){.teaching-button .teaching-button-exercise} -- Sheet: Sales
:::

![](../images/excel_sales_solution.png){fig-align="center" width="90%"}

## Slide 20 — DAX moving averages (first take-home exercise)

::: {.teaching-file}
[Take-home exercise]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-homework} [`../materials/Excel_in_class_exercises.xlsx`](../materials/Excel_in_class_exercises.xlsx){.teaching-button .teaching-button-homework} -- Sheet: Moving Average
:::

Before or after the break.

- Explain the figure, then the formula, and then ask students how the spreadsheet should look like (draft column-names, maybe where the formulas start, but not the specific formula solutions)
- Draft structure on the blackboard
- Last step: create the visualization...

![](../images/excel_moving_average_solution.png){fig-align="center" width="90%"}

## Slide 21 - IF Dialogue

Opens when selecting ribbon Formulas, Logical, IF

::: {.teaching-break}
☕ Break — 10 minutes
:::

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class demo]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-demo} [`../materials/introduction.xlsx`](../materials/introduction.xlsx){.teaching-button .teaching-button-demo} -- Sheet: simple
:::

Solution:

![](../images/excel_cost_revenue_if_simple.png){fig-align="center" width="90%"}

---

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class demo]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-demo} [`../materials/introduction.xlsx`](../materials/introduction.xlsx){.teaching-button .teaching-button-demo} -- Sheet: Cost_Revenue
:::

Solution:

![](../images/excel_cost_revenue_if_nested.png){fig-align="center" width="90%"}

## Slides 23–24 — VIP classification and credits

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class exercise]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-exercise} [`../materials/Excel_in_class_exercises.xlsx`](../materials/Excel_in_class_exercises.xlsx){.teaching-button .teaching-button-exercise} -- Sheet: VIP
:::

Prefer nested `IF` functions for the VIP-status exercise (in part II).

![](../images/excel_vip_solution.png)

## Slide 25 — Installment loan (take-home exercise)

::: {.teaching-file}
[Take-home exercise]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-homework} [`../materials/Excel_in_class_exercises.xlsx`](../materials/Excel_in_class_exercises.xlsx){.teaching-button .teaching-button-homework} -- Sheet: Installment | Annuity
:::

- Illustrate the repayment logic using the visualization: [materials/loans.html](../materials/loans.qmd).
  We could imagine that a loan adviser uses a similar interface to discuss this with her clients.
  We also note that there are some parameters that we can adjust (principal, interst, payment periods) and others that are calcualted (repayments).
  It's the same principle in this interface and in your Excel sheet.

![](../images/excel_loans_illustration.png){fig-align="center" width="40%"}

- Work through the repayment logic on the board.
- Students then transfer the calculation to Excel as a take-home exercise.
- From period 2 onward, repayment requires an `IF` condition.
- Students should identify the need for the condition themselves.
- Allow sufficient explanation because this exercise has caused difficulties in previous cohorts.

::: {.callout-important}
The repayment equals the fixed repayment amount or, when the remaining debt is lower, the remaining debt.
:::

- Insert formulas to make sure that when users modify the `amount`, `interest rate`, or `redemption`, the `payments table` is updated automatically.
- In the **annuity loan** task, the `annuity` should also be updated automatically.
- Students should have a clear understanding of what the **data cells** (manually entered/modified parameters) and the **function cells** are. (similar to Hillier and Hillier: data/changing/output/objective cells)
- Ask students to understand the `PMT(interest rate, term, loan amount)` function (payment).

::: {.callout-note title="Understanding the difference between fixed installments and anuity loans"}
With a fixed-principal installment loan, the principal is reduced more quickly at the beginning, which lowers the total interest paid. However, this also means that the initial payments are higher.
:::

## Slide 27 Exam

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class exercise]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-exercise} [`../materials/Excel_in_class_exercises.xlsx`](../materials/Excel_in_class_exercises.xlsx){.teaching-button .teaching-button-exercise} -- Sheet: Exam
:::

![](../images/excel_exam_solution.png){fig-align="center" width="60%"}

## Slide 28: Special IF functions

::: {.teaching-file}
[In-class exercise]{.teaching-label .teaching-label-exercise} [`../materials/Excel_in_class_exercises.xlsx`](../materials/Excel_in_class_exercises.xlsx){.teaching-button .teaching-button-exercise} -- Sheet: Departments
:::

Solve together (before revealing slide 28, which contains the solutions).

![](../images/excel_special_if_solution.png)

## Summary and announcements (next session)

::: {.callout-tip title="Notes for improvement"}
Take notes (improvements, common questions) on a separate paper and add to `feedback.qmd` after the session.
:::
